Patch Set 1:

Does this change make it impossible to deny the root user access to the filesystem?

I'm wondering if the better fix in this case is to add an explicit mapping for root so that it is not in the "unknown users" bucket and can be handled separately.

parents.subject: LU-17444 utils: fix fd leak after conversion to llapi_root_path_open

commit.subject: LU-17454 nodemap: root_squash independent of deny_unknown
commit.message:
LU-17454 nodemap: root_squash independent of deny_unknown

Make root_squash independent of deny_unknown, i.e. squash root to
squash_uid/squash_gid even if the deny_unknown property is set
(instead of denying access).

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I84f385db0a747d3c188fe67afe297ca211d6cc89
